items.title: Fake delivery notice
items.tagline: USPS / FedEx imposters with QR codes
items.summary: Texts claim a package is on hold and need a tiny $0.30 fee. The link leads to a clone of the carrier's site that harvests cards.
tells: Sender is a random short number, not the carrier's official short code
URL is a lookalike like usps-track-pkg[dot]top
Asks for full card details including CVV

items.title: AI-cloned voice from family
items.tagline: "Mom, I'm in trouble" but it isn't them
items.summary: Scammers harvest 3-second audio clips from social media to clone voices. They call panicked, claim arrest or hospital, ask for gift cards.
tells: Caller ID can be spoofed — call them back on a number you saved
They beg you not to tell anyone in the family
Payment requested is gift cards, crypto, or wire transfer

items.title: Tax-season impersonators
items.tagline: Robocalls and texts pretending to be IRS
items.summary: IRS never threatens immediate arrest by phone, never asks for gift cards. Real notices come by mail first.
tells: Demands gift cards, crypto, or wire
Caller refuses to send written confirmation

items.title: Romance + crypto "pig butchering"
items.tagline: Long-game scams via dating apps and Telegram
items.summary: Builds trust over weeks then pitches a private crypto platform with guaranteed returns. Early withdrawals work; large ones lock you out.
tells: Met online recently, can never video call
Pushes an obscure trading platform you can't find in app stores
Pressures you to invest more after a small "win"
